calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-21-2023 Off Calendar

Motion To Enter Default Judgment
Off Calendar Apr-05-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for motion for entry of default and prove-up hearing.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 09:21:08 a.m. and concluding at 09:36:16 a.m.

Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank represented by Adam Brumage, Esq. via Zoom Video Conference.
Defendant Travis D. Wallis not appearing.

MARIANA ECHEVERRIA-LICONA (Associate Vendor Analyst/Custodian of Records) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 1 - Electronic Recod of Account Information
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 2 - Cardholder Agreement and Changes in Terms
Plaintiff's Exhibit No. 3 - Account Statements

Court examines MARIANA ECHEVERRIA-LICONA.

The matter is submitted.
Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel, its witness, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint and Defendant is in breach of credit card agreement. Court awards Plaintiff monetary damages relevant to the credit card statement based on the last activity on the account. Court determines the sum of $34,866.34 was due on March 08-2021.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff JPMorgan Chase Bank, and against Travis D. Wallis, as follows:

1. Principal: $34,866.34

Plaintiff waives pre-judgment interest, fees, and costs.

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $34,866.34

Proposed judgment signed and filed as modified.
